excluded_deals.name: Hemorrhoid Centers of America

deals.name: CLS Health
deals.size: $3,500/mo flat + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/visit ClearPay (15–30 providers, 15–30 telemed visits/day to start)
deals.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Manager); Dr. Attia (clinical champion); CLS President (executive sponsor, engaged)
deals.status_narrative: Pricing approved internally at $3,500/mo flat + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/ClearPay. CLS is pushing back on the dual pricing structure — Abigail expected per-encounter pricing and is concerned about paying ~$18K over 6 months before telemed revenue materializes. Requesting a hybrid model (smaller platform fee + usage-based), 6-month initial term instead of 1 year, and reduced or waived $2K implementation fee. Final approval rests with the CLS president, who is actively applying pressure on Abigail's team to move. Target launch is June 1.
key_risks: Pricing model disagreement — hybrid model needs Solv finance approval before close
EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year; prospect wants contract flexibility
Payment timing workflow for telemed still unresolved (collect before or after visit?)
deals.next_step: Brantley to discuss pricing flexibility and hybrid model with Solv finance (within days of 4/23). Explore 6-month term and reduced implementation fee. Potential executive meeting with Heather (Solv CEO) to align on strategic AI partnership vision.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Pricing model pushback; president approval pending; hybrid pricing needed to close by June 1.

deals.name: GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)
deals.size: 2 locations to start (~2K calls/week); free during pilot, then $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale
deals.champion: Jim (CEO), engaged but skeptical; Deana (Clinical Manager), skeptical of AI complexity
deals.status_narrative: Pricing proposal delivered on 4/21 call — free pilot, then $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale. Jim explicitly said he needs to understand eventual pricing and ROI before committing time to the pilot. Org just 3x'd in size (14 → 40 physicians) via two recent acquisitions. No ortho reference customer remains the hardest objection; Jim softened on this over the 4/2 call but it remains a real risk.
key_risks: No ortho reference customer — Jim called this out directly, won't move without a peer reference
ROI framing is weak in low-competition market (missed calls don't necessarily mean missed revenue)
Complex scheduling logic (20+ appointment types, subspecialties, age limits) raises product fit questions
deals.next_step: War room to build an ortho-specific ROI model before next GLOC call. GLOC team responding to pricing proposal. Brantley to follow up.
deals.granola_note: No Granola meeting notes found for GLOC.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Free pilot proposed; Jim needs ROI story and ortho peer reference before committing.

deals.name: Plymouth Bay Orthopedic Associates
deals.size: 1 location (ortho urgent care, opening June 1); 3 additional ortho clinics as expansion potential; MRR TBD
deals.locations: 1 (urgent care focus); 3 additional ortho clinics in scope
deals.champion: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, ex-PhysicianOne, former Solv user); Christopher LaFave (Executive Director, decision authority)
deals.status_narrative: Strong demo on 4/8 with 4 stakeholders (Executive Director, Ops Manager, Clinical Manager, Dir. of Marketing). No major UX objections; urgent care use case is a clean fit. Team confirmed internal review and plans to reconnect in ~1 week. June 1 clinic opening is a real forcing function. ECW integration confidence is the primary gating factor — Christopher wants to see data flowing live into ECW before committing.
key_risks: ECW integration confidence is gate — they find ECW tech integrations consistently difficult
Referral workflow gap blocks expansion to the 3 other ortho clinics (HMO referral requirement)
June 1 timeline may be too tight to execute implementation cleanly
deals.next_step: Brantley to send deck + ECW integration packet (was due Mon/Tues post-4/8). Plymouth Bay team internal review, then reconnect call in ~1 week.
deals.next_step_timing: Reconnect expected week of 4/14–4/18 (may be slightly past due)
deals.granola_note: No Granola meeting notes found for Plymouth Bay Ortho.
deals.key_notes_one_line: 4-stakeholder demo landed well; ECW integration proof is the gate to close.

deals.name: Premier Ortho
deals.deal_phase: Paused (was Clear MSP / near Paper Process)
deals.champion: Jon Borja (COO), self-advocating but contractually blocked
deals.status_narrative: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon (COO) proactively communicated that contractual obligations to Assort Health block all forward movement — no timeline for resolution given. Was previously at near-Paper Process stage (DocuSign ready). Jon confirmed intent to return when clear; Brantley checking in passively every ~2 weeks.
key_risks: Assort Health contract exit has no timeline — could be weeks or months
If Assort exit drags, timeline pressure driving urgency is lost
Competitive risk if Assort resolves and another vendor moves in first
deals.next_step: Passive monitoring only. Jon holds the ball. Brantley periodic check-in (~every 2 weeks; next touch ~4/28).
deals.granola_note: No Granola meeting notes found for Premier Ortho.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Assort contract is a hard block; Jon intends to return when clear but no timeline.

deals.name: Total Primary Care
deals.size: 47 locations confirmed; acquisition-layer only (no scheduling replacement); MRR TBD
deals.champion: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
deals.status_narrative: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. TPC won't replace their home-built scheduler ('too many resources poured into it') — not on their timeline for next 6-9 months. Rikin is interested in Solv as a GMB/patient acquisition layer only, running in parallel with their existing scheduler. Also actively piloting a competing voice agent. Team should decide to park or close.
key_risks: 6-9 month self-stated timeline with no commitment — long, low-probability hold
Acquisition-layer-only scope limits deal size and ECW integration complexity is unvalidated
Rikin is actively testing a competing voice agent
deals.next_step: Team to decide: park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or close. If pursuing, validate acquisition-layer + ECW workflow feasibility first.
deals.next_step_timing: Decision needed now; re-engage Q3 2026 if parked
deals.granola_note: No Granola meeting notes found for Total Primary Care.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Not on timeline 6-9 months; acquisition-layer only; team should decide to park or close.

deals.name: Elevate Health Group
deals.status_narrative: Dr. Tovmassian pushed back hard on 3/16: 'Doesn't make sense for our practice, 90% of patients are recurring.' Current ClearPay model isn't landing for a recurring primary care patient mix. No response to follow-ups since. Pain is real (post-visit collections, IPA routing errors) but product fit isn't there. Owner also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care — risk of damaging that relationship by pushing too hard.
key_risks: Product-fit mismatch — recurring primary care model doesn't fit point-of-care estimate workflow
deals.next_step: Low-touch only. Do not push given Mederi relationship. Re-engage if ClearPay model evolves for recurring primary care patients.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Product-fit objection from owner; near dead; don't push — existing Mederi partnership at risk.

executive_summaries.cls_health:
**CLS Health** — Clear MSP | New Logo
- **Size**: $3,500/mo flat + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/visit ClearPay; 15–30 providers, 15–30 telemed visits/day to start
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Manager); Dr. Attia (clinical); President engaged as executive sponsor
- **Status**: Pricing approved internally by Solv. CLS is pushing back on the dual pricing structure — Abigail wants a hybrid model (smaller platform fee + usage-based), 6-month initial term, and reduced/waived $2K implementation fee. President is applying pressure on Abigail's team to move fast. Final approval is his. Target launch June 1.
- **Key risks**:
  - Hybrid pricing model needs Solv finance sign-off before deal can close
  - EMR switching to Epic/Oracle in ~1 year; CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
  - Telemed payment timing workflow (pre- vs. post-visit collection) still unresolved
- **Next step**: Brantley to explore hybrid pricing/6-month term with Solv finance this week. Potential exec meeting with Heather (Solv CEO). Owner: Brantley/Teresa. Due: by ~4/25.
executive_summaries.gloc:
**GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)** — Clear MSP | New Logo (Design Partner)
- **Size**: 2 locations to start (~2K calls/week); free during pilot, then $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jim (CEO), engaged but skeptical; Deana (Clinical Manager), skeptical of AI scheduling complexity
- **Status**: Pricing proposal delivered on 4/21 — free pilot, then usage-based at scale. Jim explicitly conditioned pilot participation on understanding eventual pricing and ROI. Org just 3x'd in size via two acquisitions. No ortho reference customer is the hardest objection.
- **Key risks**:
  - No ortho reference customer — Jim stated he won't move without a peer reference
  - ROI argument is weak in low-competition market; missed calls don't reliably equal missed revenue
  - Scheduling complexity (20+ appointment types, subspecialties) raises product fit risk
- **Next step**: War room builds ortho-specific ROI model before next GLOC call. Brantley follows up on pricing proposal. Owner: Brantley/Teresa/Katie. Due: this week.
executive_summaries.plymouth_bay_ortho:
**Plymouth Bay Orthopedic Associates** — Building Our Champion | New Logo
- **Size**: 1 location (ortho urgent care, opening June 1); 3 additional ortho clinics as expansion potential; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, ex-PhysicianOne, former Solv user); Christopher LaFave (Executive Director, decision authority)
- **Status**: Strong 4/8 demo with 4 stakeholders — no major objections, urgent care use case is a clean fit. Team doing internal review and targeting a reconnect in ~1 week. June 1 opening is a real forcing function. ECW integration confidence is the primary gate — Christopher wants to see live data flow before committing.
- **Key risks**:
  - ECW integration confidence is gate — this group consistently struggles with tech integrations
  - Referral workflow gap blocks expansion to the 3 other ortho clinics
  - June 1 timeline may be too tight for clean implementation
- **Next step**: Brantley to send deck + ECW integration packet (may be past due from 4/8). Plymouth Bay team internal review then reconnect call. Owner: Brantley. Due: ~week of 4/14.
executive_summaries.premier_ortho:
**Premier Ortho** — Formally Paused (was Clear MSP / near Paper Process) | New Logo
- **Size**: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and self-advocating but contractually blocked
- **Status**: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon proactively surfaced that Assort Health contract obligations block all movement — no timeline given. Was at near-Paper Process stage (DocuSign was queued) before this emerged. Brantley monitoring passively, checking in every ~2 weeks.
- **Key risks**:
  - Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal is fully stalled with no active path forward
  - Prolonged pause erodes urgency and creates competitive exposure
  - No active outreach means risk of losing Jon's attention if another vendor moves during the window
- **Next step**: Passive monitoring. Jon holds the ball. Brantley next check-in ~4/28. Owner: Brantley.
executive_summaries.total_primary_care:
**Total Primary Care** — Prospecting (Paused) | New Logo
- **Size**: 47 locations confirmed; ECW; acquisition-layer only (no scheduling replacement); MRR TBD
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
- **Status**: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. TPC won't replace their home-built scheduler — 'too many resources poured into it' — and is not on timeline for 6-9 months. Rikin is interested in Solv as an acquisition layer alongside their existing scheduler, but is also piloting a competing voice agent. Team should decide to park or close.
- **Key risks**:
  - 6-9 month timeline with zero commitment is a long, low-probability hold
  - Acquisition-layer-only scope limits deal size and ECW integration complexity is unvalidated
  - Competing voice agent pilot already underway with Rikin
- **Next step**: Team decision: park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or close out. Owner: Brantley/Teresa. Due: now.
executive_summaries.elevate_health_group:
**Elevate Health Group** — Prospecting (Near Dead) | Expansion
- **Size**: 3 locations, 18 providers; ClearPay only at ~$0.50/visit
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Katia Sarafian (admin/billing); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) skeptical
- **Status**: Dr. Tovmassian pushed back hard on 3/16 — 'Doesn't make sense for our practice, 90% recurring patients.' No response to follow-ups. Pain is real but product-fit isn't landing. Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care — pushing too hard risks that relationship.
- **Key risks**:
  - Product-fit mismatch for recurring primary care patient model
  - Dark since mid-March — effectively ghosting
  - Aggressive pursuit risks damaging the Mederi (existing partner) relationship
- **Next step**: Low-touch only. Do not push. Re-engage if ClearPay model evolves for recurring primary care. Owner: Brantley.

leadership_table.key_notes: Pricing model pushback; president approval pending; hybrid pricing needed to close by June 1.
Free pilot proposed; Jim needs ROI story and ortho peer reference before committing.
4-stakeholder demo landed; ECW integration proof is the gate to close.
Assort contract is a hard block; Jon intends to return when clear, no timeline.
Not on timeline 6-9 months; acquisition-layer only; team should decide to park or close.
Product-fit objection from owner; near dead; don't push — Mederi partnership at risk.
